title: 4 Elements of Data Protection by Design (per JPDP DPbD Guideline, April 2026)
description: The four DPbD elements adopted by JPDP in Section 4 of the issued DPbD Guideline. The issued guideline does NOT adopt Cavoukian's 7 foundational principles framework — this is a material departure from PCP No. 2/2025 and from common analyst frameworks. Privacy-as-default is folded into Element 1 (Proactiveness); Privacy-Embedded-in-Design and Positive-Sum disappear as named principles.

guidelineStatus.issuedReference: Garis Panduan Mereka Bentuk Berdasarkan Perlindungan Data (DPbD), Jabatan Perlindungan Data Peribadi, April 2026, Section 4
guidelineStatus.structuralDivergenceFromPCP: PCP No. 2/2025 listed seven foundational principles (Cavoukian framework). The issued guideline condenses to four elements. Audit narratives, control libraries, and policy documents that reference 'the 7 foundational principles' should be updated to reference these 4 elements.

elements.name: Sifat Proaktif (Proactiveness)
elements.summary: Anticipate and prevent privacy risks before they occur. Encompasses both proactive prevention (formerly Cavoukian P1) AND privacy-as-default (formerly Cavoukian P2 — folded in here).

subElements.4.2.1: Proactive prevention — privacy considerations integrated at the earliest possible stage of any processing design.
subElements.4.2.2: Privacy as the default setting — most privacy-protective configuration is the default; data subjects need not act to protect their privacy.

operationalisation: Senior leadership commitment to a strong proactive privacy programme (Section 13 governance practices)
Adequate resourcing for the privacy programme
Personal data protection compliance in senior management performance evaluation
Regular audits and DPIAs reported to the board
Default to no collection unless a specific compelling purpose is defined
Opt-in consent for non-essential processing
Pre-tick only strictly-necessary cookies
Most privacy-protective default settings across system configuration

elements.name: Perlindungan Hujung-ke-hujung (End-to-end Protection)
elements.summary: Ensure data security throughout the entire processing lifecycle — secure retention and secure destruction at end of process. No gaps in protection or accountability.
operationalisation: Encryption in transit (TLS 1.2+) and at rest (AES-256 or equivalent)
Encryption keys managed in a dedicated key management system
MFA for staff access; role-based access control with least privilege
Pseudonymisation as soon as direct identifiability no longer needed
Automated, encrypted backups with disaster recovery plan
Anonymisation and aggregation for analytics where possible
Endpoint protection; staff training on privacy and security
Clear retention policies with secure deletion methods at end of retention
Logging and audit trail with regular review

elements.name: Ketelusan (Transparency)
elements.summary: Demonstrate accountability for personal data processing. Handle data per stated promises, subject to verification. Encompasses both visibility/openness and accountability.
operationalisation: Documented privacy-related policies and procedures, assigned to a specified individual
Information about data management policies and practices made readily available
Equivalent protection ensured for third-party transfers (contractual or other)
Complaint and redress mechanisms established and clearly communicated
Just-in-time notices when data is collected (e.g., account creation, order placement)
Layered notices: key points emphasised, link to detailed information
Pop-up notices for automatically collected data (cookies, beacons, tracking scripts)
Accessible link to comprehensive notice on every web page

elements.name: Berpusatkan Pengguna (User-centricity)
elements.summary: Keep the interests of the individual paramount through strong privacy defaults, appropriate notice, and empowering user-friendly options.
operationalisation: Consent: free and specific; quality of consent scales with data sensitivity; withdrawable
Personal data accurate, complete, up-to-date as necessary
Data subjects can access, challenge accuracy, and amend their data
Complaint and redress mechanisms with appeal escalation
Privacy dashboard with clear options (toggle data points, customise preferences)
Separate consent for different processing purposes
In-app/timely notifications about policy changes before they take effect

principlesNotAdoptedFromCavoukian.note: The following Cavoukian foundational principles are NOT named in the issued JPDP guideline. They remain useful analyst frameworks but should not be cited as JPDP-issued requirements.
principles: P3: Privacy embedded into design (folded into E1 Proactiveness implicitly)
P4: Full functionality – positive sum, not zero sum (no equivalent in issued guideline)
